title: I-Team obtains email thread between SHA, city hall and developer for East Adams project
summary: The Syracuse Housing Authority failed to vote on a $2M city funding agreement for environmental work on the East Adams housing project after commissioners claimed they hadn't reviewed the resolution beforehand. However, obtained emails reveal SHA leadership and counsel were involved in all negotiations. City lawyer Susan Katzoff expressed serious concern that SHA's board action jeopardizes a $30M RCN funding deadline dependent on NEPA completion.

political_signal.description: I-Team investigative reporting exposing governance failures and miscommunication at SHA board level; potential federal funding at risk due to project delays and board dysfunction.

processing_notes: Dollar amounts need precision: $2 million for environmental work, $30 million RCN federal funding at risk
Key timeline: Special SHA board meeting April 7, 2025; email sent by Katzoff same day
NEPA (National Environmental Policy Act) completion is critical path blocker for federal funds
Conflict between SHA Executive Director's claims of non-involvement and email evidence of full participation
Consider cross-referencing with East Adams Neighborhood project records and RCN federal grant documentation
